segments.text: Most certainly, I tell you, one who doesn’t enter by the door into the sheep fold, but climbs up some other way, is a thief and a robber.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: But one who enters in by the door is the shepherd of the sheep.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: The gatekeeper opens the gate for him, and the sheep listen to his voice. He calls his own sheep by name and leads them out.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Whenever he brings out his own sheep, he goes before them; and the sheep follow him, for they know his voice.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: They will by no means follow a stranger, but will flee from him; for they don’t know the voice of strangers.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Jesus spoke this parable to them, but they didn’t understand what he was telling them.
Jesus therefore said to them again,
Most certainly, I tell you, I am the sheep’s door.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: All who came before me are thieves and robbers, but the sheep didn’t listen to them.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: I am the door. If anyone enters in by me, he will be saved, and will go in and go out and will find pasture.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: The thief only comes to steal, kill, and destroy. I came that they may have life, and may have it abundantly.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: “I am the good shepherd. The good shepherd lays down his life for the sheep.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: He who is a hired hand, and not a shepherd, who doesn’t own the sheep, sees the wolf coming, leaves the sheep, and flees. The wolf snatches the sheep and scatters them.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: The hired hand flees because he is a hired hand and doesn’t care for the sheep.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: I am the good shepherd. I know my own, and I’m known by my own;
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: even as the Father knows me, and I know the Father. I lay down my life for the sheep.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: I have other sheep which are not of this fold. I must bring them also, and they will hear my voice. They will become one flock with one shepherd.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Therefore the Father loves me, because I lay down my life, that I may take it again.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: No one takes it away from me, but I lay it down by myself. I have power to lay it down, and I have power to take it again. I received this commandment from my Father.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Therefore a division arose again among the Jews because of these words.
Many of them said,
He has a demon and is insane! Why do you listen to him?
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Others said,
These are not the sayings of one possessed by a demon. It isn’t possible for a demon to open the eyes of the blind, is it?
segments.speaker: Others

segments.text: It was the Feast of the Dedication[^26] at Jerusalem.
It was winter, and Jesus was walking in the temple, in Solomon’s porch.
The Jews therefore came around him and said to him,
How long will you hold us in suspense? If you are the Christ, tell us plainly.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Jesus answered them,
I told you, and you don’t believe. The works that I do in my Father’s name, these testify about me.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: But you don’t believe, because you are not of my sheep, as I told you.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: My sheep hear my voice, and I know them, and they follow me.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: I give eternal life to them. They will never perish, and no one will snatch them out of my hand.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: My Father who has given them to me is greater than all. No one is able to snatch them out of my Father’s hand.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: I and the Father are one.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Therefore the Jews took up stones again to stone him.
Jesus answered them,
I have shown you many good works from my Father. For which of those works do you stone me?
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: The Jews answered him,
We don’t stone you for a good work, but for blasphemy, because you, being a man, make yourself God.
segments.speaker: The Jews

segments.text: Jesus answered them,
Isn’t it written in your law, ‘I said, you are gods’?
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: If he called them gods, to whom the word of God came (and the Scripture can’t be broken),
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: do you say of him whom the Father sanctified and sent into the world, ‘You blaspheme,’ because I said, ‘I am the Son of God’?
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: If I don’t do the works of my Father, don’t believe me.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: But if I do them, though you don’t believe me, believe the works, that you may know and believe that the Father is in me, and I in the Father.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: They sought again to seize him, and he went out of their hand.
He went away again beyond the Jordan into the place where John was baptizing at first, and he stayed there.
Many came to him. They said,
John indeed did no sign, but everything that John said about this man is true.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Many believed in him there.
